data.body:
[G4 child-consistency audit — parent 72d5 (Migrate the LLM gates onto the workflow engine) × child B6 (7e48: Remove throwaway sample workflows; confirm two-real-workflow end state)]

Checked all 7 cross-child interaction modes against the WHOLE sibling roster (B1 723c, B2 6b06, B3 dc58, B4 101a, B5 f12d, B-RETIRE 961b, B-DM 5506, B-UX 92fa, 0408). ONE high-confidence finding (conflicting assumption with 0408 / the live tree). The other six modes read benign (footnoted).

═══════════════════════════════════════════════
FINDING 1 — Conflicting assumptions: B6 (CLOSED+SIGNED) asserts its end-state as "the gates run on the two real [engine] workflows," but on the live tree plan-review's gate still runs on the BESPOKE path (gate_engine default reverted to "bespoke" by PR #42), and a still-open sibling (0408) is what actually re-defaults plan-review onto the workflow engine. So B6 attests the epic's "two-real-workflow run-on-engine" end-state as confirmed while a sibling establishes that one of those two gates is NOT yet running on the engine live.
───────────────────────────────────────────────
- criteria:
  • parent AC6 / Success Criterion ("End state: exactly two real LLM workflows (plan-review, completion-verification) … run on the ONE workflow-engine substrate")
  • B6 "Why"/"What" ("Confirm … that the gates run on the two real workflows") + B6 AC3 ("End state confirmed: exactly two real LLM workflows (plan-review, completion-verification)")
  • 0408 AC4 ("verify.gate_engine default is flipped back to workflow") + 0408 AC2 (live review-plan returns a real PASS/BLOCK via the workflow engine)

- location: B6 Why/What + AC3; src/rebar/config.py (gate_engine default; reverted to "bespoke" per PR #42, per 0408 Why and B-RETIRE comment 1782505678304851001); the workflow plan-review verify/coach prompt steps (src/rebar/llm/workflow/runs.py RunnerAgentStep; gates/plan-review.yaml).

- evidence:
  • B6 is CLOSED+SIGNED; its "What"/AC3 frame the end-state as "the gates run on the two real workflows" (engine substrate).
  • The live default is bespoke: 0408's Why states PR #42 "flipped the default back to bespoke as a hotfix" because the workflow plan-review verify/coach steps fail to resolve {{plan}} live → INDETERMINATE; B-RETIRE's comment 1782505678304851001 confirms "bespoke is currently the ONLY working plan-review gate."
  • B6's own claim comment acknowledges it is "based on the hotfix branch (fix/gate-engine-default-bespoke) since it must merge after #42" — i.e. B6 lands ON TOP of a tree where plan-review runs bespoke, yet its AC3 declares the two-real-workflow run-on-engine end-state confirmed.
  • B6's completion-verification PASS read AC3 strictly as FILE INVENTORY ("No other .yaml files under examples/ or gates/") — it did NOT verify that plan-review actually runs on its engine workflow live; the "gates run on the two real workflows" half of B6's stated intent is therefore unsubstantiated against the live default.

- scenarios:
  1. A reader trusts B6 (signed, AC3 "end state confirmed") as proof the epic's two-real-workflow-on-engine end-state holds, and does not realize plan-review still dispatches through bespoke until 0408 lands — so the parent Success Criterion ("plan-review … run on the ONE workflow-engine substrate") is presented as met while it is not yet true on the live path.
  2. If 0408 is deprioritized/dropped, the epic ships with B6 signed-confirming the end-state while gate_engine stays "bespoke" — plan-review never actually runs on the engine substrate live, silently defeating the parent's central goal despite a green/signed B6.
  3. B-RETIRE (961b) — which removes the bespoke path — is gated on 0408 (its own comment), yet B6's signed "end state confirmed" could be mistaken as evidence the engine path is already the working gate, tempting premature bespoke removal.

- impact: The sibling set is internally inconsistent on the load-bearing end-state: B6's signed AC3 ("two real LLM workflows … confirmed" / "the gates run on the two real workflows") reads as the engine-substrate end-state being reached, but the live tree has plan-review on bespoke (0408 still open to re-default to workflow). B6's normative ACs are actually only about FILE INVENTORY (delete code_review.yaml + templates.py; retain review_skeleton.yaml; exactly two workflow YAMLs + one sample) — that part is sound and verified — so the inconsistency is confined to the "and the gates RUN on the two real workflows" phrasing in B6's Why/What overreaching the live state. This is a real consistency artifact (same root cause as the B2/0408 {{plan}}-plumbing gap), not a duplication of independent work.

- checklist_item: "Check the 7 cross-child modes … conflicting assumptions … each detected mode is a finding."

- suggested_fix: Scope B6's confirmation explicitly to INVENTORY and decouple it from the run-on-engine claim: (a) reword B6's Why/What + AC3 to "exactly two real LLM workflow FILES exist (plan-review.yaml, completion-verification.yaml) + the one retained sample; no throwaway stubs remain," and DROP/qualify "the gates run on the two real workflows" so B6's signature does not assert a live-engine end-state it did not verify; (b) cross-reference that the "plan-review runs on the engine substrate LIVE (default=workflow)" half of the parent end-state is delivered by 0408 (tepid-bus-pomp), not B6, so no reader treats B6's signed AC3 as proof plan-review is off bespoke; (c) ensure the parent's AC6 end-state checkbox is keyed off 0408's completion (default re-flipped to workflow), not B6's inventory confirmation.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════
Modes read benign (no finding):

(1) Implicit shared state — B6 mutates shared surfaces: src/rebar/llm/workflow/__init__.py (drop templates re-exports), _cli/_workflow_commands.py (inline the scaffold), and the bound test files. No sibling concurrently mutates these post-cutover; B6 sequences itself after B5/PR #42 on the hotfix branch. Acknowledged, not hidden.

(2) Conflicting assumptions (other) — captured as Finding 1.

(3) Dependency gap — B6 depends on B5 ("cutover complete, so the bespoke samples are truly unused"). Minor rationale mismatch: code_review.yaml + templates.py are throwaway EXAMPLE/scaffold stubs, not the bespoke plan-review path, so their deletion does not strictly depend on the gate cutover — but B6's real gating concern (merge AFTER PR #42 on the hotfix branch, per its claim comment) is sound, and B-RETIRE (961b) correctly KEEPS passes.py etc. that the workflow still uses. No actual missing dependency.

(4) Scope overlap — B6 RETAINS review_skeleton.yaml; B-UX (92fa) / B-DM (5506) CONSUME it as the visual-editing refinement sample. Distinct ownership (retain vs. consume), not duplicated. The "epic A's demo workflow" in parent AC5 maps correctly to examples/review_skeleton.yaml (epic A story A3, per parent comment 1782414477060576001). Benign.

(5) Ordering violation — B6 depends on B5 and blocks B-RETIRE (961b) (dep present: 7e48 blocks 961b). Correctly positioned after cutover, before bespoke-path removal. Consistent.

(6) Consumer impact — B6 deletes templates.py (consumed by `rebar workflow new` via _workflow_commands.py:221 + __init__.py re-export) and code_review.yaml (consumed by test_bpmn.py:398, test_workflow_schema_golden.py:78, test_code_review_example.py, README.md:164, schema.py:424 docstring). B6 explicitly preserves `rebar workflow new` (inline scaffold) and lists all three bound test files + __init__.py in its file_impact; README/docstring reference the retained `rebar workflow new` command (not the deleted files). No unhandled consumer break.

(7) Residual references — B6 AC2 requires grep-clean; its completion-verification asserts no live references to code_review.yaml/templates.py/SCAFFOLD_V1 remain (the only hit is a documenting comment inside review_skeleton.yaml). The retained review_skeleton.yaml is preserved, so no dangling reference to a removed symbol. Benign.

ANTI-FP note: Finding 1 is reported as a single high-confidence finding because the live tree OBJECTIVELY contradicts B6's signed end-state framing — gate_engine default was reverted to "bespoke" (PR #42; corroborated by 0408 Why + B-RETIRE comment 1782505678304851001 "bespoke is currently the ONLY working plan-review gate"), and B6's own claim comment concedes it merges on top of fix/gate-engine-default-bespoke — so "the gates run on the two real [engine] workflows" is not true live, while a separate open sibling (0408) owns flipping it back. The remaining six modes are filtered out under the benign-reading rule.
